raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.3,"RX.5":0.3},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Could you clarify whether you are seeking feedback primarily on the technical validity and novelty of the described framework, or on the writing style and presentation?","rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation 'What do you think?' implies a request for general feedback on a paper draft, making verification of the argument's logical consistency a central expected responsibility.","R1.3":"Given the artifact's focus on evaluation metrics and claims, assessing evidence-claim alignment is central to providing meaningful feedback on the draft.","R1.4":"Asking 'What do you think?' about a research description likely expects an estimation of the work's novelty against prior art as a key component.","R1.6":"The artifact contains lengthy sentences and could benefit from polish, so sentence-level writing improvements are a central aspect of potential feedback.","RX.1":"The delegation invites overall thoughts, making disclosure of uncertainties in the draft's claims a central cross-cutting concern.","RX.2":"To provide responsible feedback, avoiding overclaims in the artifact's presentation is centrally important.","RX.3":"Ensuring the draft adheres to its stated scope is a central element in evaluating the described framework.","RX.4":"While not core to the delegation, considering potential downstream harms from the anomaly detection system is a peripheral nice-to-have for thoroughness.","RX.5":"Tracking provenance of data and methods in the draft is peripheral but relevant for completeness in feedback."}}
